expectations.text: All 4 decomposition artifacts created: execution-backlog.md, coverage-matrix.md, decomposition-review-package.md, stage-5-handoff.md — each with all required template sections
expectations.evidence: Only 1 of 4 required artifacts was created: execution-backlog.md. The three missing artifacts are coverage-matrix.md, decomposition-review-package.md, and stage-5-handoff.md. The outputs directory contains only execution-backlog.md.

expectations.text: Subtasks cover all 6 modules from the change map: auth, tenant, user, config, migrations, server — no module orphaned
expectations.evidence: All 6 modules are covered: auth (Tasks 2.3 keycloak_client, 3.2 sso_service, 4.1 handler), tenant (Tasks 1.4 model, 2.1 repository), user (Tasks 1.5 model, 2.2 repository, 3.1 service), config (Task 1.3 SSOConfig struct), migrations (Tasks 1.1 tenant_sso_config table, 1.2 users.keycloak_id column), server (Task 4.2 route registration in cmd/server/main.go).

expectations.text: Foundation subtasks (migrations, config, shared types) are in Wave 1 with no blocking dependencies
expectations.evidence: Wave 1 contains 5 foundation tasks all with 'Dependencies: None': Task 1.1 (migration tenant_sso_config), Task 1.2 (migration users.keycloak_id), Task 1.3 (SSOConfig struct), Task 1.4 (TenantSSOConfig model), Task 1.5 (User.KeycloakID field). The document explicitly states 'These subtasks have zero dependencies on each other and can all be executed in parallel.'

expectations.text: Dependencies correctly reflect that SSO service depends on tenant repository, user service, Keycloak client, and config
expectations.evidence: Task 3.2 (SSO Service) lists dependencies as: 'Task 2.1 (tenant repo), Task 2.3 (keycloak client), Task 3.1 (user service SSO methods), Task 1.3 (config)'. This correctly captures all four required dependencies. The dependency graph section also shows '3.2 SSO Service ← 2.1, 2.3, 3.1, 1.3'.

expectations.text: Each subtask has explicit boundaries (in-scope/out-of-scope), completion criteria, and related design decisions
expectations.evidence: Subtasks have 'What' (brief scope), 'Work' (implementation details), 'Done when' (completion criteria), and 'Dependencies' sections. However, there are no explicit 'in-scope/out-of-scope' boundary sections — scope is implied but not formally delineated. There are also no 'related design decisions' sections referencing design choices from earlier pipeline stages. Completion criteria are present via 'Done when' sections.

expectations.text: Decomposition critic subagent was spawned and returned DECOMPOSITION_APPROVED
expectations.evidence: No evidence of a decomposition critic subagent anywhere in the output. The execution-backlog.md contains no review verdicts, approval stamps, or any reference to a critic process. Only a single artifact was produced with no quality-gate metadata.

expectations.text: Coverage reviewer subagent was spawned and returned COVERAGE_OK with at least medium confidence
expectations.evidence: No evidence of a coverage reviewer subagent anywhere in the output. There is no coverage-matrix.md, no COVERAGE_OK verdict, and no confidence rating. The acceptance criteria section at the bottom of execution-backlog.md lists criteria as checkboxes but does not constitute a coverage review.

expectations.text: Coverage matrix maps all 7 acceptance criteria from agreed-task-model.md to specific subtasks
expectations.evidence: No coverage-matrix.md file exists. The execution-backlog.md has an 'Acceptance Criteria' section listing 8 feature-level criteria as checkboxes, but these are not mapped to specific subtasks. The 7 acceptance criteria from agreed-task-model.md (SP-initiated SSO, password login unchanged, JIT provisioning, auto-linking, per-tenant config, Keycloak automation, JWT claims) are listed but without traceability to individual tasks.
